title: Unit 24 Learn Greek Grammar Masterclass: Reflexive Pronouns (Myself, Yourself)

script.text: Welcome... to the Learn Greek Grammar Masterclass for Unit 24: Reflexive Pronouns. In English, we say 'myself', 'yourself', 'himself'. In Greek, we have one core word to do all that work. I'm William, and I'm joined by Athina. Athina... who is this mysterious character we use for reflexive actions?
Literally... 'The self of mine'. We treat our 'self' like a separate masculine noun that we own.
'The self of mine'. That’s actually very deep. So instead of just a pronoun, it’s a noun-phrase. How do we say 'I see myself'?
is the Accusative form because it’s the object of the seeing.
What if I wanted to say 'You should take care of yourself'?
Πρέπει να φροντίζεις τον εαυτό σου.
Notice that only the possessive pronoun at the end changes to
τον εαυτό μου, σου, του... μας, σας, τους.
It’s very consistent. But Athina, what happens if the 'Self' is the one doing the possession? Like 'My self's problem'?
The problem of my self. It sounds a bit formal, but it’s perfectly correct.
Now... pro tip: We often use reflexive pronouns with verbs that don't have a deponent or passive form to mean 'doing it to oneself'. Instead of a passive verb, we use Active + Reflexive.
I understand myself. You wouldn't use a passive form there. It’s the most direct way to express self-reflection.
It’s like having a mirror in your grammar. This concludes Unit 24. You now know how to talk about the most important person in your life: yourself!
Τα λέμε στο επόμενο μάθημα. Γεια σας!
